Interface RenderCanvas

All Superinterfaces:
AutoCloseable, NonThrowingAutoCloseable
All Known Subinterfaces:
RenderCanvasExt
All Known Implementing Classes:
GLRenderCanvas, GLSubCanvas
public interface RenderCanvas extends NonThrowingAutoCloseable
A canvas for 2D rendering operations.

This interface provides methods for drawing shapes and images, applying transformations, setting styling properties, and managing clipping regions. It maintains a state stack that can be saved and restored using save() and restore().

Note: When images and shapes are uploaded into GPU memory, they're typically cached using identity semantics (comparing by reference, not value), so custom renderers are expected to cache constantly used images/shapes and treat them as immutable objects to prevent data consistency issues.

Coordinate System

The coordinate system uses floating-point coordinates where (0, 0) is typically the top-left corner of the rendering surface. Positive X extends right and positive Y extends down.

Note for Scratchers: This is different from Scratch's coordinate system. In Scratch, (0, 0) is the center of the stage, positive X values extend right and positive Y values extend up. To get the center coordinates, use getDimensions() to get the width & height of the canvas and divide these values by 2. Vanilla renderers handle conversion between these systems automatically; custom renderers must do this manually.

Graphics State

The render canvas maintains a state that includes:
  • Current transformation matrix (translation, rotation, scaling)
  • Current fill and stroke colors
  • Line width
  • Alpha (transparency) value
  • Blend mode
  • Graphic effects
  • Current clipping region
This state can be pushed onto the stack with save() and popped & restored with restore().

Color Representation

Colors are represented as ARGB integers where:
  • Bits 0-7: Alpha (opacity) component (0 = fully transparent, 255 = fully opaque)
  • Bits 8-15: Red component
  • Bits 16-23: Green component
  • Bits 24-31: Blue component

Blend Modes

Blend modes control how new pixels are combined with existing pixels in the framebuffer. Available modes include:
  • BlendMode.ADD: Adds color components together
  • BlendMode.BLEND_SUBTRACT: Subtracts color components (true subtractive)
  • BlendMode.RGB_SUBTRACT: Subtracts RGB components, adds alpha (layered subtractive)
  • BlendMode.BLEND_MULTIPLY: Multiplies all components (true multiplicative)
  • BlendMode.RGB_MULTIPLY: Multiplies RGB components, adds alpha (layered multiplicative)
See ARGB for utility methods to work with ARGB colors, an explanation of layered/true blending types, and more info about ARGB format.

Resource Management

The render canvas may hold native resources that should be released by calling close().
See Also:
  • ARGB
  • BlendMode
  • NonThrowingAutoCloseable

  • Field Summary

    Fields
    Modifier and Type
    Field
    Description
    static final double
    ORIGINAL_SIZE
    Pass this constant to width / height arguments of drawImage(Image, double, double, double, double) to prefer the original width / height of the image.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    void
    clip(double x, double y, double w, double h)
    Sets the current clipping region to the given rectangular path.
    void
    close()
    Releases any resources associated with this render canvas.
    RenderCanvas
    createSubCanvas(double width, double height)
    Creates a new sub-canvas that renders into an offscreen buffer.
    void
    drawImage(Image image, double x, double y, double w, double h)
    Draws an image at the specified position with the given size.
    default void
    drawImage(Image image, Point2D topLeft, Point2D deltaSize)
    Vector version of drawImage(Image, double, double, double, double).
    default void
    drawLine(double x1, double y1, double x2, double y2)
    Draws a line from one position to another as specified in drawShape(Shape).
    default void
    drawLine(Point2D first, Point2D second)
    Vector version of drawLine(double, double, double, double).
    default void
    drawOval(double x, double y, double w, double h)
    Draws an outline of an oval with the given position and dimensions as specified in drawShape(Shape).
    default void
    drawOval(Point2D topLeft, Point2D deltaSize)
    Vector version of drawOval(double, double, double, double).
    default void
    drawRect(double x, double y, double w, double h)
    Draws an outline of a rectangle with the given position and dimensions as specified in drawShape(Shape).
    default void
    drawRect(Point2D topLeft, Point2D deltaSize)
    Vector version of drawRect(double, double, double, double).
    void
    drawShape(Shape shape)
    Draws the outline of the specified shape using the current stroke color, line width and blend mode.
    default void
    fillOval(double x, double y, double w, double h)
    Draws an outline of an oval with the given position and dimensions as specified in drawShape(Shape) and fills it as specified in fillShape(Shape).
    default void
    fillOval(Point2D topLeft, Point2D deltaSize)
    Vector version of fillOval(double, double, double, double).
    default void
    fillRect(double x, double y, double w, double h)
    Draws an outline of a rectangle with the given position and dimensions as specified in drawShape(Shape) and fills it as specified in fillShape(Shape).
    default void
    fillRect(Point2D topLeft, Point2D deltaSize)
    Vector version of fillRect(double, double, double, double).
    void
    fillShape(Shape shape)
    Draws an outline of the specified shape as specified in drawShape(Shape) and fills it using the current fill color and blend mode.
    RenderingContext
    getContext()
    Returns the parent context of this canvas.
    Point2D
    getDimensions()
    Returns a vector representing the width and height of the canvas area.
    default boolean
    isSubCanvas()
    Checks whether this canvas is a sub-canvas.
    void
    mulTransform(AffineTransform at)
    Multiplies the coordinate system by the specified affine transform matrix.
    void
    resetClip()
    Resets the clipping region to the default (typically the entire rendering surface).
    void
    restore()
    Restores the most recently saved graphics state from the stack.
    void
    rotate(double degrees)
    Rotates the coordinate system around the origin by the specified angle (subsequent drawing operations will be rotated).
    void
    save()
    Saves the current graphics state (transformations, styling, clipping) onto a stack.
    void
    scale(double sx, double sy)
    Scales the coordinate system by the specified factors.
    default void
    scale(Point2D delta)
    Scales the coordinate system by the specified delta vector.
    void
    setAlpha(byte alpha)
    Sets the alpha (transparency) value for all drawing operations.
    void
    setBlendMode(BlendMode mode)
    Sets the blend mode for combining source pixels with destination pixels.
    void
    setEffects(GraphicEffectMap effects)
    Sets the graphic effects map to apply to subsequent drawing operations.
    void
    setFillARGB(int argb)
    Sets the fill color for shape filling operations.
    void
    setIdentity()
    Resets the current graphics state to default values.
    void
    setLineWidth(double width)
    Sets the line width for stroke operations.
    void
    setStrokeARGB(int argb)
    Sets the stroke color for shape outline drawing operations.
    default void
    submitImage(double x, double y, double w, double h)
    Draws the final rendered image on the parent canvas as described in drawImage(), if this canvas is a sub-canvas.
    default void
    submitImage(Point2D topLeft, Point2D deltaSize)
    Vector version of submitImage(double, double, double, double).
    void
    translate(double dx, double dy)
    Translates the coordinate system by the specified amounts (subsequent drawing operations will be offset by (dx, dy)).
    default void
    translate(Point2D delta)
    Translates the coordinate system by the specified delta vector.

  • Field Details

    • ORIGINAL_SIZE

      static final double ORIGINAL_SIZE
      Pass this constant to width / height arguments of drawImage(Image, double, double, double, double) to prefer the original width / height of the image.
      See Also:
      • Constant Field Values

  • Method Details

    • drawImage

      void drawImage(Image image, double x, double y, double w, double h)
      Draws an image at the specified position with the given size.

      If either dimension is set to ORIGINAL_SIZE, then the image's respective dimension is used.

      Parameters:
      image - the image to draw
      x - the X coordinate of the top-left corner
      y - the Y coordinate of the top-left corner
      w - the preferred width of the image or ORIGINAL_SIZE
      h - the preferred height of the image or ORIGINAL_SIZE
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if image is null
      IllegalArgumentException - if dimensions are negative, or if any argument is infinite or NaN (ORIGINAL_SIZE for coordinates doesn't count since it's a special value)
      See Also:
      • drawImage(Image, Point2D, Point2D)

    • fillShape

      void fillShape(Shape shape)
      Draws an outline of the specified shape as specified in drawShape(Shape) and fills it using the current fill color and blend mode.
      Parameters:
      shape - the shape to fill
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if shape is null
      See Also:
      • drawShape(Shape)
      • setFillARGB(int)
      • setBlendMode(BlendMode)

    • drawShape

      void drawShape(Shape shape)
      Draws the outline of the specified shape using the current stroke color, line width and blend mode.
      Parameters:
      shape - the shape to draw
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if shape is null
      See Also:
      • setStrokeARGB(int)
      • setLineWidth(double)
      • setBlendMode(BlendMode)

    • drawImage

      default void drawImage(Image image, Point2D topLeft, Point2D deltaSize)
      Vector version of drawImage(Image, double, double, double, double).
      Parameters:
      image - the image to draw
      topLeft - the top-left corner coordinates
      deltaSize - the preferred dimensions of the image
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if any argument is null

    • fillRect

      default void fillRect(double x, double y, double w, double h)
      Draws an outline of a rectangle with the given position and dimensions as specified in drawShape(Shape) and fills it as specified in fillShape(Shape).
      Parameters:
      x - the X coordinate of the top-left corner
      y - the Y coordinate of the top-left corner
      w - the width of the rectangle
      h - the height of the rectangle
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if dimensions are negative, or if any argument is infinite or NaN
      See Also:
      • drawShape(Shape)
      • fillShape(Shape)

    • fillRect

      default void fillRect(Point2D topLeft, Point2D deltaSize)
      Vector version of fillRect(double, double, double, double).
      Parameters:
      topLeft - the top-left corner coordinates
      deltaSize - the size dimensions of the rectangle
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if any argument is null
      IllegalArgumentException - if dimensions are negative, or if any argument is infinite or NaN

    • drawRect

      default void drawRect(double x, double y, double w, double h)
      Draws an outline of a rectangle with the given position and dimensions as specified in drawShape(Shape).
      Parameters:
      x - the X coordinate of the top-left corner
      y - the Y coordinate of the top-left corner
      w - the width of the rectangle
      h - the height of the rectangle
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if coordinates or dimensions are negative, infinite or NaN
      See Also:
      • drawShape(Shape)

    • drawRect

      default void drawRect(Point2D topLeft, Point2D deltaSize)
      Vector version of drawRect(double, double, double, double).
      Parameters:
      topLeft - the top-left corner coordinates
      deltaSize - the size dimensions of the rectangle
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if any argument is null
      IllegalArgumentException - if dimensions are negative, or if any argument is infinite or NaN

    • drawLine

      default void drawLine(double x1, double y1, double x2, double y2)
      Draws a line from one position to another as specified in drawShape(Shape).
      Parameters:
      x1 - the X coordinate of the first position
      y1 - the Y coordinate of the first position
      x2 - the X coordinate of the second position
      y2 - the Y coordinate of the second position
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if any argument is infinite or NaN
      See Also:
      • drawShape(Shape)

    • drawLine

      default void drawLine(Point2D first, Point2D second)
      Vector version of drawLine(double, double, double, double).
      Parameters:
      first - the first position
      second - the second position
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if any argument is null
      IllegalArgumentException - if any argument is infinite or NaN

    • fillOval

      default void fillOval(double x, double y, double w, double h)
      Draws an outline of an oval with the given position and dimensions as specified in drawShape(Shape) and fills it as specified in fillShape(Shape).
      Parameters:
      x - the X coordinate of the top-left corner
      y - the Y coordinate of the top-left corner
      w - the width of the oval
      h - the height of the oval
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if dimensions are negative, or if any argument is infinite or NaN
      See Also:
      • drawShape(Shape)
      • fillShape(Shape)

    • fillOval

      default void fillOval(Point2D topLeft, Point2D deltaSize)
      Vector version of fillOval(double, double, double, double).
      Parameters:
      topLeft - the top-left corner coordinates
      deltaSize - the size dimensions of the oval
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if any argument is null
      IllegalArgumentException - if dimensions are negative, or if any argument is infinite or NaN

    • drawOval

      default void drawOval(double x, double y, double w, double h)
      Draws an outline of an oval with the given position and dimensions as specified in drawShape(Shape).
      Parameters:
      x - the X coordinate of the top-left corner
      y - the Y coordinate of the top-left corner
      w - the width of the oval
      h - the height of the oval
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if dimensions are negative, or if any argument is infinite or NaN
      See Also:
      • drawShape(Shape)

    • drawOval

      default void drawOval(Point2D topLeft, Point2D deltaSize)
      Vector version of drawOval(double, double, double, double).
      Parameters:
      topLeft - the top-left corner coordinates
      deltaSize - the size dimensions of the oval
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if any argument is null
      IllegalArgumentException - if dimensions are negative, or if any argument is infinite or NaN

    • save

      void save()
      Saves the current graphics state (transformations, styling, clipping) onto a stack. The state can be restored later using restore().
      See Also:
      • restore()
      • translate(double, double)
      • rotate(double)
      • scale(double, double)

    • restore

      void restore()
      Restores the most recently saved graphics state from the stack. This reverts all transformations, styling, and clipping to their values when save() was last called.
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if the state stack is empty
      See Also:
      • save()

    • setIdentity

      void setIdentity()
      Resets the current graphics state to default values. Useful for finer control over texture/primitive position, rotation, scale, etc.

    • translate

      void translate(double dx, double dy)
      Translates the coordinate system by the specified amounts (subsequent drawing operations will be offset by (dx, dy)).
      Parameters:
      dx - the distance to translate along X axis
      dy - the distance to translate along Y axis

    • translate

      default void translate(Point2D delta)
      Translates the coordinate system by the specified delta vector.
      Parameters:
      delta - the translation amount vector point
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if delta is null

    • rotate

      void rotate(double degrees)
      Rotates the coordinate system around the origin by the specified angle (subsequent drawing operations will be rotated).

      Note for Scratchers: In Scratch, the rotation angle is relative to the upper part of the vertical axis (0 degrees is facing up, 90 degrees is no rotation, etc.). In the context of graphics, for convenience’s sake (and because there's usually no front face like with Scratch's built-in costumes), a different system is used: 0 degrees means no rotation, positive values rotate clockwise and negative values - counterclockwise. Conversion between these systems happens automatically in vanilla classes, but custom renderers need to do this manually.

      Parameters:
      degrees - the angle of rotation in degrees (0 or multiples of 360 mean no rotation)

    • scale

      void scale(double sx, double sy)
      Scales the coordinate system by the specified factors. Subsequent drawing operations will have their coordinates multiplied by (sx, sy).
      Parameters:
      sx - the scaling factor for the X axis
      sy - the scaling factor for the Y axis
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if sx or sy is/are 0, infinite or NaN

    • scale

      default void scale(Point2D delta)
      Scales the coordinate system by the specified delta vector.
      Parameters:
      delta - the scale factor vector point
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if delta is null
      IllegalArgumentException - if any vector component is 0, infinite or NaN

    • mulTransform

      void mulTransform(AffineTransform at)
      Multiplies the coordinate system by the specified affine transform matrix.
      Parameters:
      at - the transform matrix
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if transform matrix is null

    • setFillARGB

      void setFillARGB(int argb)
      Sets the fill color for shape filling operations. This color will be used by subsequent calls to fillShape(Shape) and convenience methods.
      Parameters:
      argb - the ARGB color value (see ARGB)
      See Also:
      • fillShape(Shape)
      • setBlendMode(BlendMode)
      • ARGB

    • setStrokeARGB

      void setStrokeARGB(int argb)
      Sets the stroke color for shape outline drawing operations. This color will be used by subsequent calls to drawShape(Shape) and convenience methods.
      Parameters:
      argb - the ARGB color value (see ARGB)
      See Also:
      • drawShape(Shape)
      • setBlendMode(BlendMode)
      • ARGB

    • setLineWidth

      void setLineWidth(double width)
      Sets the line width for stroke operations. This width will be used by subsequent calls to drawShape(Shape) and convenience methods.
      Parameters:
      width - the line width in coordinate units (must be positive)
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if width is non-positive, infinite or NaN

    • setAlpha

      void setAlpha(byte alpha)
      Sets the alpha (transparency) value for all drawing operations. This value is combined with the alpha components of fill/stroke colors. A value of 0 means fully transparent, 255 (-1) means fully opaque.
      Parameters:
      alpha - the alpha value (0-255 unsigned)
      See Also:
      • setFillARGB(int)
      • setStrokeARGB(int)

    • setBlendMode

      void setBlendMode(BlendMode mode)
      Sets the blend mode for combining source pixels with destination pixels. The blend mode affects all drawing operations including shape filling/drawing and image drawing.
      Parameters:
      mode - the blend mode to use
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if mode is null
      See Also:
      • BlendMode
      • drawImage(Image, double, double, double, double)
      • fillShape(Shape)
      • drawShape(Shape)

    • setEffects

      void setEffects(GraphicEffectMap effects)
      Sets the graphic effects map to apply to subsequent drawing operations.

      Note: Spatial distortion effects can't be applied to vector content (shapes, text); effects that can be applied to them are: HUE_SHIFT, BRIGHTNESS and FADE. Use createSubCanvas(double, double) and submitImage(double, double, double, double) to apply spatial distortion to vector elements.

      Parameters:
      effects - the effect map

    • clip

      void clip(double x, double y, double w, double h)
      Sets the current clipping region to the given rectangular path.
      Parameters:
      x - X coordinate of top-left corner
      y - Y coordinate of top-left corner
      w - width
      h - height
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if w or h is/are non-positive, or if any argument is negative, infinite or NaN
      See Also:
      • resetClip()
      • translate(double, double)
      • rotate(double)
      • scale(double, double)

    • resetClip

      void resetClip()
      Resets the clipping region to the default (typically the entire rendering surface). After calling this method, all drawing operations will not be clipped (except by the bounds of the rendering surface itself).
      See Also:
      • clip(double, double, double, double)

    • createSubCanvas

      RenderCanvas createSubCanvas(double width, double height)
      Creates a new sub-canvas that renders into an offscreen buffer.

      All drawing operations on the returned canvas are isolated and rasterized into a procedural image when closed. The result can be drawn onto the parent canvas via submitImage(double, double, double, double).

      This enables:

      • Coherent application of graphic effects
      • Layered rendering
      • Reusable render passes
      Example: 
      try (RenderCanvas layer = canvas.createSubCanvas(200, 200)) {
    layer.setFillArgb(ARGB.RED);
    layer.fillRect(10, 10, 50, 50);
    layer.drawOval(30, 30, 40, 40);
    // If "canvas" has graphic effects, they'll be applied to the whole image, not to each component separately!
    layer.submitImage(100, 100, 200, 200);
}
      Parameters:
      width - the width of the sub-canvas
      height - the height of the sub-canvas
      Returns:
      a new render canvas targeting an offscreen buffer
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if either dimension exceeds this canvas's respective dimension or is non-positive

    • isSubCanvas

      default boolean isSubCanvas()
      Checks whether this canvas is a sub-canvas. If true, then submitImage(double, double, double, double) can be safely called.
      Returns:
      true for sub-canvases, false for the main canvas

    • submitImage

      default void submitImage(double x, double y, double w, double h)
      Draws the final rendered image on the parent canvas as described in drawImage(), if this canvas is a sub-canvas.
      Parameters:
      x - the X coordinate of the top-left corner
      y - the Y coordinate of the top-left corner
      w - the preferred width of the image or ORIGINAL_SIZE
      h - the preferred height of the image or ORIGINAL_SIZE
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if this canvas is not a sub-canvas
      See Also:
      • isSubCanvas()

    • submitImage

      default void submitImage(Point2D topLeft, Point2D deltaSize)
      Vector version of submitImage(double, double, double, double).
      Parameters:
      topLeft - the top-left corner coordinates
      deltaSize - the preferred dimensions of the image
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if this canvas is not a sub-canvas
      See Also:
      • isSubCanvas()

    • getDimensions

      Point2D getDimensions()
      Returns a vector representing the width and height of the canvas area.
      Returns:
      the dimensions of this canvas

    • getContext

      RenderingContext getContext()
      Returns the parent context of this canvas.
      Returns:
      the parent context of this canvas

    • close

      void close()
      Releases any resources associated with this render canvas. After calling this method, the canvas should not be used. Calling this method more than once does not have any effect.
      Specified by:
      close in interface AutoCloseable
      Specified by:
      close in interface NonThrowingAutoCloseable
